title: 10 best herbal teas for gum infection
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum infection are infusions made from plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties, which can help to reduce gum inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing. Examples of herbal teas that can help with gum infection include chamomile, calendula, echinacea, and sage. These herbs contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and promote tissue regeneration. By drinking these herbal teas, people with gum infection can experience relief from pain and discomfort, reduce the risk of further infection, and promote the healing of gum tissue. This can lead to improved oral health, fresher breath, and a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with gum infection because its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial growth that causes inflammation and redness in the gums. The tea's flavonoids, specifically kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a common bacteria linked to gum disease. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum infection, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Sage tea contains antimicrobial compounds like thujone, camphor, and bornyl acetate that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in sage tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage symptoms like bleeding and tooth sensitivity.

Antioxidant activity: Sage tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the mouth from oxidative stress, reducing damage caused by free radicals that can contribute to gum disease development and progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, bark helps combat bacterial growth and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sage leaves measure out one teaspoon per cup.
Boil filtered water in a teapot or kettle bring to a boil within five minutes.
Add the measured sage leaves to the boiling water steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup or mug using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and sweeten with honey or sugar if desired serve warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with gum infection because its active compound, calendula, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that effectively combat bacterial and fungal infections in the gums. The tea's antibacterial agents also reduce plaque buildup and prevent further damage to the gums, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. As a result, pot marigold teas can help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, such as swelling, redness, and bleeding, and support overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids and carotenoids that have antibacterial effects, which can help combat bacteria that cause gum infections like gingivitis and periodontitis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids can reduce inflammation in the gums, which is a common symptom of gum infections, and promote healing.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidants can help protect the mouth from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain antibacterial compounds that help combat gum infection-causing bacteria.
Seeds: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in morning dew when petals are fully open.
Rinse the flowers gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers with boiling water.
Steep mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors and nutrients to infuse into tea.
Strain tea through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the production of biofilms, allowing for a reduction in the bacterial load on the gums. By reducing inflammation and combating infection-causing bacteria, thyme teas can help alleviate symptoms of gum disease and promote healing of the affected areas.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, help combat bacteria that cause gum infections like gingivitis and periodontitis, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in thyme tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling, pain, and redness associated with gum infections, promoting healing and comfort.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gum disease and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Thymus vulgaris have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing gum infections and reducing swelling.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain essential oils like thymol and carvacrol, which possess antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections causing gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves, selecting those with vibrant green color and no signs of wilting or browning, approximately one ounce for each cup of tea desired.
Rinse the harvested thyme leaves gently with cool filtered water to remove any dirt debris, then pat dry with a clean cloth to prevent contamination.
Combine the washed thyme leaves with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic teapot, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes or until desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large serving pitcher or individual cups, discarding the solids and reserving the flavorful liquid for consumption.

Allow the tea to cool to room temperature if preferred, or serve warm immediately with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a touch of honey if desired, approximately 20-25 minutes from start to finish.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with gum infection because its active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help combat the bacteria that cause gum disease, reducing inflammation and swelling in the gums. Additionally, the teas' antibacterial properties may also help prevent further bacterial growth, promoting healing and tissue repair in affected areas. This natural remedy has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various oral health issues, including gum infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: The tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as echinacein and isobutylamides, help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of gum tissue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea purpurea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cichoric acid, which may reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Immune system modulation: The tea's immunomodulatory effects, particularly its ability to increase the production of certain cytokines, may help boost the body's natural defense against infections, promoting a healthy balance between inflammation and healing in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections associated with gum disease.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of saponins and polyphenols, rhyzomes help to soothe irritated gums and fight against oral pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the collected botanicals with clean water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Combine one teaspoon of dried echinacea petals with 8 ounces of boiling filtered water steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discard solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then sweeten with honey to taste and serve warm within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with gum infection because of its natural antibacterial properties, which have been shown to effectively combat bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The tea's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms in the gums, ultimately promoting healthy oral flora and reducing symptoms associated with gum infection, including swelling and bleeding.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: A bioactive compound found in St. John's Wort, hyperforin has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial infections and reduce swelling in the gums.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have been shown to possess antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in St. John's Wort tea have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby preventing tissue damage and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth.
Bark: The bark of St. John's Wort contains antioxidants and flavonoids that may help soothe and calm gum tissue, reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hypericum perforatum flowers from reputable sources.
Measure two teaspoons of dried st john's wort flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured herbs in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes before straining and serving.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose teas helps with gum infection because it is a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and pain in the gums. The tea's antimicrobial properties also combat bacteria responsible for infections, promoting healing and preventing further damage. Additionally, dog rose teas contain antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress, which can exacerbate gum disease. As a result, regular consumption of dog rose teas may help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, promoting healthy and strong teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dog rose tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to gum infection and disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dog rose tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Antimicrobial properties: Dog rose tea contains antimicrobial compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, which is a common causative agent of gum infections, thereby helping to prevent the spread of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing gum infections.
Bark: Contains tannins, which reduce inflammation and aid in healing infected gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dog rose petals from a pesticide-free environment.
Rinse the harvested petals with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried dog rose petals or 40-60 grams of fresh petals with boiling water in a pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Transfer the infused tea to a cup, add honey to taste if desired, and enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with gum infection because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has antimicrobial properties. This compound can help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to the gums and bone. Additionally, licorice tea's anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infections, promoting a faster recovery and healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which helps to reduce the growth and spread of bacteria that can cause gum infections.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums and surrounding tissues caused by infection.
Licoricidin: This compound has antiviral properties, which can help to combat viral infections that may contribute to gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Bark: Licorice bark is used to make medicinal teas as it contains flavonoids and saponins, which have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, helping to combat gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root for every eight ounces water.
Heat one quart filtered water in a medium saucepan to a gentle boil.
Steep the measured root in the hot water for twelve to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool and settle for two to five minutes before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry teas helps with gum infection because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the swelling and pain associated with gum infections. The anthocyanins present in elderberries have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including Streptococcus mutans, which is a common cause of gum disease. Additionally, elderberry's antimicrobial properties can help to combat fungal and viral infections that may contribute to gum inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Elderberry tea contains antimicrobial compounds like anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, including those that cause gum disease.
Inflammation reduction: The polyphenols present in elderberry tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with gum infection and inflammation.
Antioxidant activity: Elderberry tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the mouth from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease and infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infections.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the bark has antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria causing gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late summer.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried elderberries with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release antioxidants and flavonoids.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Serve hot or chilled, sweetened with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The antioxidants present in plantain also help to combat bacterial growth, which can contribute to the development of gum disease. Additionally, plantain's soothing properties calm the gum tissue, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, thereby aiding in the natural resolution of infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.

Antimicrobial properties: Plantain tea contains antimicrobial agents that can inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, which are responsible for gum infections, thereby promoting healing.
Wound-healing properties: Plantain tea has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound closure rates, making it effective in promoting the repair and healing of damaged gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum infections.
Bark: The bark contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help dry up pus and reduce bleeding in infected gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves ensure cleanliness.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold water remove impurities.
Steep the leaves in boiling water allow for infusion.
Strain and sweeten as desired enhance flavor.
Enjoy your herbal plantain tea soothing benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with gum infection because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe swollen gums, reducing redness and discomfort. The tea's mucilages also create a protective barrier that coats the mouth and throat, shielding against bacteria and irritants. Additionally, marshmallow root has natural antibacterial and antifungal agents that combat infections and promote healthy gum tissue growth, ultimately helping to heal and prevent recurrent gum infections.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tea contains high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance that soothes and covers the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the gums.

Antimicrobial properties: Marshmallow tea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections, helping to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in marshmallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting a faster recovery from gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing swelling and discomfort.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains mucilages that can protect and coat the affected area, providing a barrier against irritants and bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots dig up 20-30 cm of root system in spring or fall avoiding mature plants.
Clean and dry cut the harvested roots into small pieces remove excess soil and let air dry for 24 hours.
Grind grind dried althaea officinalis roots into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5-10 minutes.
Infuse combine 1 teaspoon of ground roots with 250ml boiling water steep for 5-7 minutes then strain through cheesecloth or tea strainer.
Serve pour the infused liquid into a cup add honey to taste if desired enjoy immediately after preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal teas is to use a strong infusion of antimicrobial herbs, such as sage, calendula, or echinacea. Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then rinse your mouth with the cooled tea several times a day. Regular use can reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing. However, it's important to also maintain good oral hygiene and seek professional dental care if symptoms persist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal teas include inflammation and soreness of the mouth and throat. Herbal teas with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, such as chamomile, sage, and licorice root, can help alleviate symptoms of gum infection and other mouth-related ailments. Chamomile tea has soothing properties that can reduce inflammation and provide relief from pain, while sage tea has antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help heal wounds and reduce infection. Licorice root tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help relieve pain and inflammation in the mouth. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief for minor ailments, they should not be used as a substitute for professional medical or dental treatment.
